text.title: Sunset Customer Comms Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Sunset Customer Comms Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Cause sentence MUST be plain language; corporate-speak ('exciting chapter', 'evolving') is rejected.
rule.rationale: Customers can read corporate-speak and resent being patronised at the moment of loss.

rule.statement: Migration window MUST be ≥30 days; shorter windows are rejected unless legal force majeure.
rule.rationale: 30 days lets users export data, evaluate alternatives, and dispute charges without chargebacks.

rule.statement: A single named human MUST sign the comms; 'team' / 'we' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; pivot moments need a face.

rule.statement: Migration path MUST be one of {v2, competitor, refund, hybrid}; 'we'll figure it out' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous migration paths produce chargebacks and reputation damage.

rule.statement: Announce MUST hit ≥3 channels (email + in-app + status-page) so no user misses it through inattention.
rule.rationale: Single-channel announces miss ~30% of the user base.
